creator: erwin@FreeBSD.org
text:
On Tue, Jan 06, 2015 at 02:31:31AM +0000, bapt@freebsd.org wrote:
> > It has already been explained a couple of time, a port need a MASTER_SITE                                                                                                                       
> > which is not distcache                                                                                                                                                                          
>                                                                                                                                                                                                   
> It has already been STATED a number of times. And each time it was stated, it                                                                                                                     
> was countered by the fact, that distcache is more stable than an average                                                                                                                          
> master-site. This was the conclusion of our discussion on the matter, as far as                                                                                                                   
> I remember it.                                                                                                                                                                                    
>                                                                                                                                                                                                   

To summarise previous discussions:

distcache is exactly what it says in the name: a cache.  It is not an
authoritative site that can be used as a master site.  If it is more
stable than the average master site, it is exactly to have a fallback if
a master site is temporarily unavailable, but it cannot in itself be a
master site. It's as simple as that.
